data.title: Triple Flag outlines 2025 sales guidance of 105,000–115,000 GEOs while expanding royalty portfolio
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Triple Flag Precious Metals Corp. (TFPM) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Sheldon Vanderkooy, CEO, reported "another record quarter in Q2," with sales of nearly 29,000 GEOs, record adjusted EBITDA of $76 million, and record operating cash flow of USD 0.38 per share. He stated the company is "well to deliver our 2025 guidance of 105,000 to 115,000 ounces over the balance of the year."
  • The CEO described a "solid pace of accretive acquisitions" in 2025, highlighting investments in the Tres Quebradas lithium mine, Arcata and Azuca silver mines, and a new 1.5% gross revenue royalty on the Johnson Camp copper mine, with all three expected to deliver first revenue in the second half of the year.
  • The acquisition of a 1% NSR royalty on the Arthur Gold project in Nevada was completed, with the CEO calling it an asset that "offers exceptional long-term growth potential, underpinned by a rapidly expanding resource base and significant exploration upside."
  • Vanderkooy noted progress at Westgold's Beta Hunt mine, where a maiden resource for the Fletcher zone of 2.3 million ounces was declared, nearly doubling the resource base at Beta Hunt.
  • The CEO announced the "fourth consecutive annual increase of our dividend since we listed in 2021."
  • CFO Eban Bari stated, "operating cash flow per share... has increased by over 50% year-over-year to a new quarterly record."
  • Bari added, "Our dividend has been increased to USD 0.23 on an annualized basis, up 5% from prior dividend."
  • The CFO reported, "we exited the quarter with 0 debt" and expect "to be in net cash position by the end of Q3 at current metal prices," describing liquidity of nearly $1 billion.
  • Bari highlighted that "revenues derived 100% from precious metals with roughly 2/3 from gold" and that "90% of our revenue was derived from assets in Australia and the Americas."

Outlook

  • Management reiterated 2025 sales guidance of 105,000 to 115,000 GEOs.
  • The CEO stated, "we had a strong first half in 2025 and are well positioned to achieve 2025 guidance over the remainder of the year."
  • No changes to guidance were communicated compared to the previous quarter, and the CEO expressed confidence, saying, "even if we don't get a single ounce from ATO, I'm still comfortable with our guidance for 2025."
  • Several catalysts were mentioned, including production commencements at Johnson Camp Mine, Arcata, and Tres Quebradas and development progress at Northparkes, Koné, Hope Bay, and Arthur projects.

Financial Results

  • Nearly 29,000 GEOs were sold in Q2, with a record adjusted EBITDA of $76 million and record operating cash flow of USD 0.38 per share.
  • The CFO reported a record first half of over 57,000 GEOs.
  • The dividend was increased to USD 0.23 on an annualized basis, up 5% from the prior dividend.
  • The company exited the quarter with zero debt and nearly $1 billion in liquidity.
  • Northparkes and Cerro Lindo were identified as the two largest revenue contributors, with Northparkes having a record quarter due to higher open pit grades and Cerro Lindo benefiting from higher silver prices.

Q&A

  • Joshua Mark Wolfson, RBC Capital Markets: Asked about expected production from the Johnson Camp royalty purchase and steady-state production estimates. Sheldon Vanderkooy replied, "I don't believe we're going to be giving any like asset-specific guidance on that, but it's a little bit of incremental copper exposure."
  • Wolfson asked about the process for enforcing Triple Flag's security on the ATO stream and the operator's credit situation. Vanderkooy explained, "The current amount of the arrears that Steppe owes us is circa USD 8 million. So it's not a tremendous amount of money... We are in dialogue with Steppe. They understand our position very clearly, and I really can't say anymore as it's a legal dispute."
  • Wolfson followed up on assumed production from ATO in 2025 guidance. Vanderkooy responded, "even if we don't get a single ounce from ATO, I'm still comfortable with our guidance for 2025."
  • Fahad Tariq, Jefferies LLC: Inquired about potential offsets for lower production expected at Northparkes and Cerro Lindo in 2026. Vanderkooy pointed to new production from Arcata and Johnson Camp mines as potential offsets, but did not provide asset-specific guidance.
  • Tariq also asked about deal flow, pipeline focus, and views on corporate M&A. Vanderkooy described the pipeline as "really full" and stated, "our sweet spot remains in the $100 million to $300 million range."
  • Tanya M. Jakusconek, Scotiabank: Asked if the pipeline is mainly precious metals and about the mix of development versus producing assets. Vanderkooy confirmed the pipeline is "predominantly" precious metals and includes a "good number of actual producing opportunities."
  • Jakusconek clarified whether streams would involve equity or debt participation. Vanderkooy replied, "No, I wouldn't assume that at all... We prefer to focus our investment dollars on streams and royalties."
  • Jakusconek asked about geographic focus. Vanderkooy confirmed, "Definitely, the focus is Americas and Australia."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were neutral to slightly positive, focusing on specifics of guidance, asset contributions, and deal pipeline, but pressed for more detail on enforcement of the Steppe Gold stream and production offsets.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, repeatedly expressing comfort with guidance and the company’s financial position, using phrases such as "I feel very comfortable with our guidance for 2025" and emphasizing a "robust portfolio."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst sentiment shifted from focusing on share buybacks and Steppe Gold prepay recovery to pipeline and asset-level queries, while management remained consistently confident and focused on robust performance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2025 GEO sales remained unchanged at 105,000 to 115,000 ounces.
  • The company moved from announcing the acquisition of the Orogen Royalties Expanded Silicon project in Q1 to completing the Arthur Gold (formerly Expanded Silicon) royalty in Q2.
  • Dividend increased from $0.21 annualized in Q1 to $0.23 in Q2.
  • Management tone remained confident, with a continued focus on high cash flow and maintaining a strong, debt-free balance sheet.
  • Analysts shifted from questions about share buybacks and Prieska project in Q1 to more questions on new deals, production offsets, and enforcement actions in Q2.

Risks and Concerns

  • The main challenge discussed was the legal dispute regarding Steppe Gold’s arrears of approximately USD 8 million, with the CEO stating, "I feel very comfortable with our position. We are in dialogue with Steppe."
  • The company is not providing asset-specific production guidance, which was noted in analyst questions.
  • Management consistently emphasized focus on mining-friendly jurisdictions, with most of the pipeline in the Americas and Australia.

Final Takeaway

Triple Flag Precious Metals reported a record quarter with strong GEO sales, robust operating cash flow, and an increased dividend, while maintaining a debt-free balance sheet and significant liquidity. Management reiterated full-year guidance and expressed confidence that the company is positioned for continued growth, supported by a diversified portfolio and a robust pipeline focused on accretive acquisitions in mining-friendly jurisdictions.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Singapore Exchange reports FY results
data.description:
• Singapore Exchange  press release (OTCPK:SPXCF): FY2025 adjusted net profit increased 15.9% year-on-year to S$609.5 million.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was up 16.9% at S$832.0 million, while adjusted earnings per share was 57.0 cents (49.2 cents).
  • Net revenue increased 11.7% Y/Y to S$1,298.2 million, driven by higher revenue from all business segments.

data.title: Stock index futures inch up amid tech gains; tariff concerns linger
data.description:
Stock index futures ticked up on Friday, amid a rally in tech stocks that boosted market sentiment, even as investors continued to weigh the impact of Donald Trump's tariffs.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.3%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.3%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was flat at 4.26%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 3.75%.

Wall Street's major averages on Thursday closed mixed, as positive sentiment sparked by U.S. President Donald Trump's chip tariff exemptions and progress on Russia-Ukraine de-escalation petered out.

"Markets have been mixed over the past 24 hours, with the S&P 500 edging -0.08% lower, partly weighed down by disappointing earnings from Eli Lilly, even as the Nasdaq (+0.35%) and the Mag-7 (+0.40%) reached new all-time highs," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

Trump's reciprocal tariffs took effect at midnight on Thursday, and traders will keep a watch on the impact the trade policies have going ahead.

"On the trade front, Trump’s tariffs officially came into effect yesterday, though markets showed little reaction," Reid said.

In other news, Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller is reportedly gaining momentum in the process to succeed Jerome Powell as chair of the central bank.

There is not much economic data on Friday. St. Louis Fed President Alberto Musalem will speak in an event hosted by Mississippi Valley State University.

data.title: ECN Capital reports Q2 results
data.description:
• ECN Capital press release (OTCPK:ECNCF): Q2 adjusted net income applicable to common shareholders of $10.0 million or $0.04 per share (basic), versus $7.2 million or $0.03 per share (basic) for the previous three-month period.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $31.5 million, versus $25.5 million for the previous three-month period and $31.5 million for the prior year comparable period.

data.title: European shares mixed as investors eye corporate results, trade talks
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.05%

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.31% 

France (CAC:IND)  +0.12%

The unemployment rate in France was unchanged at 7.5% in Q2 2025, in line with market expectations.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) rose +0.13% on Friday, on track to close the week with strong gains driven by upbeat corporate earnings and renewed optimism over potential progress in resolving the Ukraine–Russia conflict. 

Investors also weighed changing global trade dynamics after President Trump’s broad retaliatory tariffs took effect Thursday.

Among stocks, Novo Nordisk (NVO) extended the rally, Novartis (NVS) gained, and Siemens went up 1% while SAP lost 1%. 

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 1 basis point at 4.25%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point at 2.65%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points at 4.57%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)
